transcript.text: Rainy Monday for me.
Rainy Monday in Vancouver?
Rainy Monday in Vancouver, which is pretty typical for Vancouver, so I shouldn't complain.
Fair enough.
What's the temperature down to over there?
What is it?
It's not, it doesn't feel too cold today, I would say.
Like, we haven't really been having, oh, it's 11 degrees Celsius.
I don't know what that is in Fahrenheit.
I have to look that up.
I was it's, like, pouring here.
I think this whole area is just getting, like, swamped with rain for the next few days.
But it is not cold at 60 degrees here.
Yeah, I was looking up the temperature.
It's about the same as it is in Houston, so Houston does not get cold, like, ever.
So pretty, pretty much the same here.
And how was, how was y'all's weekend?
Did you do anything fun or just kind of chill?
It was good.
It was more of a chill.
We can't because I know that the next month of weekends won't be as chill.
What about yours?
Yeah, it was actually my birthday on Saturday.
So thank you.
Thank you.
pretty busy weekend.
I was in SF last week.
We had hosted an office or sorry, hosted an event in our office on Thursday.
And then I flew back on Friday, had dinner with my fiance.
And then on Saturday, I had a bunch of my friends over to watch college football.
So Sunday was like, I don't want to do anything.
Sure.
Well, that's exciting.
At least you're early enough in December where people are still really excited to do things for your birthday.
I feel like people who are end of December really get the short end of the stick for birthdays.
I feel like I'm right at the cutoff, you know, any later and then you lump in Christmas with the birthday for sure.
A nice sweet spot.
Cool.
Well, it's great to see you both again.
know it's been a minute and we were just sort of I think
Figuring out if the partnership made sense on both sides.
I'm glad to hear that, you know, Seema was able to review some of the content we created for Abnormal with folks like Jack and, you know, Michael in addition to yourselves.
And really what I wanted to do today was, I guess, one, see if you had any questions or concerns that you wanted me to talk through.
And then from there, I could just sort of give you a list of the next steps that we'll work on heading into the kickoff.
And then also like an overview of week by week, what the strategy sprint will consist of.
So, yeah, I figured I'd open it up to you both if you have any questions to start off.
I think for me, the questions are everything that you just kind of discussed that you're going to go over.
I just wanted to know kind of what the next steps look like, what we should expect.
But I think you're going to go through all of that.
So I think I'm good for questions for now.
What about you, Katie?
Yeah, that was pretty much all that's top of mind for me.
Perfect.
Well, I guess the first place I wanted to start was just like aligning on the content that we would create, right?
I know in like our initial conversation, you talked a lot about, right, like a small marketing team trying to keep pace with like all the content that needs to get created.
Today, y'all are doing like sales enablement, product pages, content for different products launches, but not a ton on like the SEO GE side.
And so that's the lane where we typically come in and execute is looking at your competitors.
I know you'd mentioned like whiz in the past, but seeing like, what are all the keywords where there's an opportunity for us to get quick wins?
You know, search volume, keyword difficulty, competitor content, and then really being audience first around who are we writing this content for?
And what topics does Panther want to have authority in?
So early on, I would say we focus on volume, right?
We focus on where is there, like, data that can point us to an opportunity to get visibility?
And we really just try to make sure we can get to a streamlined place with the review.
How does that feel just in terms of, like, where we would fit in and what that would open up time for you all to focus on?
Yeah, I guess my question – oh, go ahead.
Go ahead, Katie.
Oh, go ahead.
I was just going to say my question on that is, are you coming in and auditing our website and stuff, and then you let us know where you think you can kind of provide the best content?
Or is that something that we come to you and we say, hey, we want content in this and this area, and then you would just produce for us?
We would do all the heavy lifting there.
But if it's a conversation and collaboration, that typically, like, makes us more successful because you have the nuance that's not going to be in the data, right?
Like, we know what to – we know which keywords to target and what we should create content about.

But you have some of those deeper insights around like why you win against certain competitors or why like one of the topic clusters that we identify might be a more important starting point than another.
So that's how we would collaborate.
Okay.
Yeah, I wanted to mention we've started using Profound.
Are you familiar with it?
Yeah, I'm very familiar with Profound.
Yeah, we've started using that in the last like, I would say six weeks.
So we've plugged in like our personas, the topics we want to track.
So we've started doing some prioritizing in terms of like, what are these terms that we want to start focusing on?
So I feel like, yeah, I assume that that can be helpful to like feed into what we work on.
Definitely, definitely.
How's your experience been using the platform?
Has it been easy to use?
Do you feel like?
Like, it helps you with figuring out, like, what topics and prompts to track?
Or is it more about, like, content ideation that you find useful for?
piece has been the most helpful, I think.
Like, as I've been using it more, I'm spending more time actually looking at the topics and the prompts themselves.
But initially, it's just like, okay, there's just this, like, call for more content, basically.
So this has given me, like, a clear checklist of where to start.
So, like, the brief that it creates for prompts has been really helpful as far as content creation.
And then the optimization piece of just, like, the quick wins for existing content.
Like, what we can change.
Like, the slug or the format with more bullets and, like, some FAQs and stuff like that.
That's been the most helpful so far.
That makes sense, yeah.
We typically hear that, like, the AI visibility is, like, the biggest use case for the platform.
And that if you use it to actually generate.
Generate drafts.
The quality isn't the best is what we've heard.
Have you tried like generating drafts for full articles?
Yeah, the draft that so I did like a brief for I was writing like a very like, I wanted it to be like a really good piece of SEO geo content on like a use case that a lot of people have for Panther.
And the brief was like very long and thorough.
And then we did a webinar on the same topic.
So I took the transcript and the Profound brief, wrote my own prompt with those, and all our personas and stuff and use Claude to write it.
And then I also had generated it with Profound just to see how it was.
it was like, it also was lacking all the context.
Like I can't give it the additional context on the webinar.
Like there's like a field to add more instructions, but it's like limited to 500 characters or something.
So like as a content generation tool, I don't think it's great, but as like a content.

Brainstorm and like the brief, I feel like it's really helpful that and we have a lot of technical people at Panther who leadership has said, hey, let's get them writing some content, giving them a brief would be really helpful to say, hey, we just need you to write content that needs these requirements.
So, yeah, I think the briefs are really valuable.
And then just the visibility tracking.
That makes a lot of sense.

I would say that with our process, it's like the content generation is where you really see an advantage because of the way that we do agentic research, because of the way that when we're drafting an article, we can feed as much context into that as we need.

We'll have like a brief, right, as part of a distinct step in our workflow, and then we'll run everything against our fact checker agent after it's been drafted, run it all against your writing guidelines after it's been drafted and make additional changes.
So that makes sense.
And just food for thought, you're also going to get a lot of the prompt and AI visibility tracking through our partnership.
So there'll be like a product that you'll be able to use that we're launching soon.
And that'll be something that you can compare to Profound to see, you know, how it works.
is it, I wasn't sure entirely, like, obviously, I know you guys are very, very valuable in the content generation piece.
And then I wasn't sure about all those other features, what the overlap was.
And I think, I think when there was that uncertainty on budget, if we could work with you guys, that's when we bought Profound so that we'd have something to get started with right away.
So, yeah, I've been curious, like, about the overlap.
Yeah, there'll definitely be some overlap, but it should all be good things, I think, for sure.
And then hopefully you can just choose whichever, you know, product between R's and Profound's, like, works better for you.
But still take advantage of, you know, five.
Articles per week, which is what we focus on doing on the content execution side.
In terms of the strategy sprint, I've got a little presentation that we can walk through together, but this is an idea of what to expect week by week.
So in the beginning, right, we're really just trying to have our kickoff call and a product deep dive so that we can generate the artifacts around your company, your audience, your brand voice.
And also so that we can generate the content OS is what we call it.
So the roadmap of assignments and the topics where we think it makes the most sense to create a ton of different content assignments.
And then from there, we're going to move into more of a calibration stage where we're going to create our calibration article.
We'll go through the review process to make sure that we feel good about the quality that we're hitting before we start really like executing.
Executing on those assignments at scale, and then we'll get things into more of an operating cadence where we can really move towards, right, the two to three to even five articles per week.

And so by the end of the partnership, just because we want to make sure that you feel really comfortable with the quality and the direction that we're going down in terms of what we're going to create content for, you could expect about five articles by the end of the strategy sprint.
But you'll have complete clarity in terms of, like, what we would focus on creating over the next three months, even the next six months, moving into phase two.
Do you have any questions at a high level about our week-by-week roadmap for the strategy sprint?
I think so.
So would we meet weekly?
Yes, yes.
So we'll set up the kickoff call with, like, the core team that we would work with.
And then the product deep dive is going to be, like, the best.

People to give us a demo of the product, not because we need a demo in that call, but we really just want to go super deep with the folks who basically like are either product marketing or like understand, live and breathe the platform and what it's used for.
And so some things that really help us out is ahead of the kickoff, we typically are getting access to Google Analytics, Google Search Console.
This allows us to do all the analytics and reporting for you and also your CMS so that we can stage and publish and basically you don't have to worry about that once a draft has been approved.
Do you know who from the team would set us up with access to those three platforms?
I can set you up with the Google platforms.
We can get someone set you up with the CMS.
Perfect.
And who is like the web person who publishes content today?
We kind of, we have a team that we work with that helps with the website, but like Katie and I can go in and publish as well.
So any of the blogs that you see right now, Katie probably went in and published all of them.
Yeah.
Okay, nice.
And which content management system are you using?
Framer.
Framer.
Awesome.
We know Framer.
lot?
Okay.
Yeah, yeah.
We have worked with Framer a decent amount.
It's definitely a good like first CMS, you know?
Just, I have strong thoughts on because we had WordPress before and we migrated to Framer.
I'm sure things are a little easier on Framer.
A little bit.
I guess I just have a lot of strong feelings about like the limitations on Framer that I haven't encountered in other CMS platforms.
I think one of the things that is the most finicky and weird is how like if you publish a page.
So
The website, it publishes every update that's been made across the website.
So, like, when our web agency is, like, redesigning, we've been redesigning the whole website page by page.
Like, when they're doing it, they'll call, like, a deployment freeze.
So they don't want anybody publishing to the website while they're putting in staging and finalizing it.
So sometimes, like, we'll have a blog post we want to get out right away, and then we can't.
That's, like, the one little thing we'll have to, like, coordinate on.
Oh, gotcha.
So when are they typically doing those deployment freezes?
Is that, like, weekly?
Is it random?
It's kind of random.
And how long would it last for?
Sometimes it's, like, a full day.
Okay.
So we just need to make sure that when the next one is coming up, we know about it, and we just plan around that.
But you would prefer to have us stage and publish, or would you prefer to do that yourself?
I think it'd be great to have you guys do it.
And it was just, like,
I'm that we have to track, but we have a Slack channel that we use just to track that.
So maybe whoever is doing that on your side, we can add to the Slack channel so that they can see if it's in a freeze or whatever.
Or maybe we can even set up some kind of bot to just send a message to our shared Slack channel.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's been like a tedious process.
Although none of these should be like super timely, whereas sometimes we're like the threat research stuff.
We want to publish right now because it's very timely and that's when I get pressure.
That makes sense.
And I'll share a link to our shared Slack channel here in the chat.
I think, yeah, either I haven't sent this out to you all yet or you haven't joined, but definitely join that shared Slack and I'll follow up over email.
But that's going to be where all of our communication, collaboration, it's not in a meeting happens.
in meeting meeting happens.
a a happens.
So we'd love to get y'all in that shared Slack channel pretty soon here.
Are you able to share it with our .io?
Because we have two email instances.
We have .com and .io.
We can't join if you send it to our .com.
Okay, got it.
Is it the same email, but with .io?
Yes, yes.
If you want to share it with .io.
just able to use that link.
Were you?
For me, it doesn't let me.
It just makes me go to that TSC workspace, and then that gets stuck.
Yeah, let me go ahead and just invite your emails directly here.
That'll be a good idea.
And I'll go ahead and do the Panther.io.
was able to join.
Really?
Yeah.
Okay, I just sent you, Ana, an invite to your .io, so that should work.
Thank you.
No problem.
sign on .io.
It's such a weird little, another little idiosyncrasy.
Yeah, no.com was expensive, so they didn't buy it right away.
I figure, you know, that's Panther.com, so it's not a made-up word.

In terms of resources that help our team get a head start, anything that y'all want to share in the Slack channel or over email that are brand guidelines, voice and style guides, any sort of, like, personas or positioning that you've put together, product marketing one-pagers.
It doesn't have to be, like, everything that I've sent, but just, like, think about it this way, right?
Anything that helps us get up to speed on your products and services, your business, your brand, like, those are the resources to share.
And more is better than less.
So feel free to, like, just shoot.
Shoot us a bunch of links if you feel like that's the best way for us to get up to speed.
Oh, yeah, we have a lot of stuff we can share.
And then other than that, I guess I just wanted to understand for the review process, you both would be the first round of review.
And then when would things go to Jack versus Michael as like the technical review?
I guess it depends on the piece of content and if it needs to go that far.
don't foresee Jack having to review too much or like having the capacity to review too many pieces.
I feel like if it goes through Katie, a lot of the times that should be enough unless it's, I don't know, something that needs to go higher.
What do you think, Katie?
Yeah, I think depending on what content we write, like what the topics are and how in-depth it goes, some of them I can handle and then some.
We do have a lot of people outside of just Michael, so we can kind of put together like a brigade of technical reviewers.
We've done this in the past.
We had like a freelancer who we gave a group of like eight people so that it was always rotating.
So someone would have like one a month instead of a bunch.
So I on my end, I can kind of put together the list of people that we want to ask and have them like up to speed.
But yeah, Jack, think he's sometimes just his priorities shift, but I think Michael would probably be pretty consistent.
Yeah, I think the key here is that this is typically like the biggest bottleneck if we have one to driving results.
And I would say it's over engineering the review process when we might not need to, right?
Like if it's more technical, if it's mentioning the product, 100%, that would make sense.
But if we're just trying to tackle a topic that has a good amount of search volume and is relevant to the audience.

We just want to make sure that we don't turn into word babysitters, you know, like when the goal is, okay, we want to show up in AI search and the use of this adjective versus this adjective or omitting this sentence, right, versus including it, it's not going to make a difference when it comes to, you know, driving visibility.
So if we can optimize and streamline the review process during the strategy sprint, then we can definitely hit the five articles per week in phase two.
not include Jack in this list of people.
Yeah.
And we'll lean on y'all for the best, like, review process.

I'm just sort of putting this out there up front so that we don't run into, like, a bottleneck unnecessarily because we try to get, like, more people into the review process that maybe didn't need to be in it.
I see what you're saying.
Yeah.
Cool.
I think that makes sense.
Then let's just have me be the person who reviews them.
And then if I...
If need to tap someone in, I can tag Michael or someone like, because it would likely be like affection or something like that versus the whole thing.
Perfect.
That works for me.
Well, yeah, I guess from here, we will bring you into the Slack channel.
I'll send an email that has the week by week breakdown, the list of resources that are helpful for us to review.
And then as soon as we get the signature from, I believe it's Will that's signing.
Yep.
As soon as we get the signature, we'll reach out to schedule the kickoff call in January.
And we're starting the first week of January.
So right after the new year.
Very exciting.
We're very excited.
I will, we'll follow up with Will to see, make sure the signature gets done.
I need to find that email thread again, but we'll follow up with him.
Perfect.
Well, happy to jump on another call if it's helpful.
Just let me know what you need.
But appreciate your patience.
know we've been dealing with like a lot.
All interest and haven't really gotten to like a demo proof of concept point in our process yet.
So I appreciate you guys just being excited about the partnership.
And I know that you will have a lot of bandwidth to work on the content that you already have to deal with and not have to think about like the SEO and GEO use cases.
Since we'll just take that off your plate.
Awesome.
Thank you so much.
All right.
Thanks, y'all.
Talk soon.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on kickoff next steps and the strategy sprint roadmap.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=135.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Focus:** GrowthX will own SEO/GEO content to drive visibility, freeing Panther's team for sales enablement and product launches.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=181.0)
  - [**Process:** A 3-phase sprint (Kickoff → Calibration → Operating Cadence) will build a content OS, targeting 5 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [**Review:** Katie will be the primary reviewer to prevent bottlenecks; she will tap technical experts only for specific fact-checks.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)
  - [**Logistics:** Panther will provide platform access (GA, GSC, Framer) and brand assets; GrowthX will manage publishing, coordinating around Framer's deployment freezes.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy & Scope

  - [**GrowthX Focus:** SEO/GEO content to drive visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=181.0)
      - [**Method:** Analyze competitors (e.g., Wiz) and data (search volume, keyword difficulty) for quick-win opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=208.0)
      - [**Collaboration:** GrowthX provides data-driven topics; Panther adds nuance (e.g., competitive wins) for prioritization.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [**Panther's Use of Profound:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
      - [**Value:** Excellent for content ideation, brief generation, and optimization of existing content.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=354.0)
      - [**Limitation:** Poor for full article generation due to context limitations (e.g., 500-character prompt limit).](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Generation Advantage:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=504.0)
      - [**Method:** Uses agentic research and fact-checker agents to feed unlimited context into drafts, ensuring quality.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
      - [**Overlap:** GrowthX will provide a similar prompt/AI visibility tool for comparison with Profound.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=541.0)

### Strategy Sprint Roadmap

  - [**Goal:** Build a "Content OS" (roadmap) and publish \~5 articles by sprint end.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
  - [**Phase 1: Kickoff & Deep Dive**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=605.0)
      - [Kickoff call with core team.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=708.0)
      - [Product deep dive with product marketing for platform nuance.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=716.0)
      - [**Output:** Brand/audience artifacts and the Content OS.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=616.0)
  - [**Phase 2: Calibration**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=643.0)
      - [Publish one "calibration article" to align on quality and process before scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=643.0)
  - [**Phase 3: Operating Cadence**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
      - [Scale production to 2–3 articles/week, with a goal of 5/week.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)

### Logistics & Process

  - [**Platform Access:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
      - [**Google Analytics & Search Console:** Ana to provide access.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
      - [**Framer (CMS):** Ana to coordinate access.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
  - [**Publishing Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=827.0)
      - [**Owner:** GrowthX will stage and publish approved content.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=893.0)
      - [**Constraint:** Framer's deployment freezes (up to 1 day) block publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=827.0)
      - [**Coordination:** GrowthX will join the Panther Slack channel tracking freezes to plan around them.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=893.0)
  - [**Review Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1088.0)
      - [**Problem:** Over-engineering reviews is the primary bottleneck to scaling content.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)
      - [**Solution:** Katie is the primary reviewer. She will tap technical experts (e.g., Michael) only for specific fact-checks, not full article reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1253.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Ana:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
      - [Follow up with Will to ensure contract signature.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1282.0)
      - [Provide GrowthX access to Google Analytics and Search Console.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
      - [Coordinate Framer CMS access for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=740.0)
  - [**Katie:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
      - [Share brand guidelines, personas, and other resources via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1038.0)
      - [Compile a list of technical reviewers for specific fact-checks.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1130.0)
  - [**Tyler:**](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
      - [Send email with sprint roadmap and resource list.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1271.0)
      - [Schedule January kickoff call after contract is signed.](https://fathom.video/share/k4qSTVSXWx9Jr5MWE6hG_kZExpHcCjSq?tab=summary&timestamp=1282.0)
